title: Information Assurance & Security
term: UT San Antonio — Summer 2026 — John Newsom

cta.text: New to the course? Complete Start Here first →

narrativeIntro.tagline: This course is structured as a year-long consulting engagement.
narrativeIntro.body:
You’re a Junior Analyst at NEXUS Security, a fictional Texas-based cybersecurity consulting firm. Over five modules you’ll work four real-world client engagements — Brazos Financial, Gulf Coast Healthcare, Alamo Industries, and LoneStar DevOps — spanning the full breadth of analyst work: reconnaissance, cryptography, network analysis, threat assessment, and risk management.

Every module produces an Engagement Packet — the same kind of deliverable a real consulting firm bills a client for. You’ll track your hours, document your methodology, and write client-facing reports backed by primary sources. The work is graded on the standard professional deliverables are held to, not on busywork.

This is a purple team course. You’ll learn offensive techniques (how attackers find weaknesses) and defensive thinking (how organizations close them) in parallel. The tools, frameworks, and reporting practices you build here are the same ones used in the field — and the same ones evaluated by UTSA’s cybersecurity competition teams. Learn more about NEXUS Security and meet the team you’ll work with.

clients.name: Brazos Financial Group
clients.engagement: External reconnaissance and password audit for a regional bank facing growing fraud pressure.

clients.name: Gulf Coast Healthcare
clients.engagement: Certificate remediation and network analysis ahead of a HIPAA compliance audit.

clients.name: Alamo Industries
clients.engagement: Active Directory password assessment for a defense-industry manufacturer modernizing its OT environment.

clients.name: LoneStar DevOps
clients.engagement: Vulnerability assessment and risk prioritization for a fast-growing startup’s cloud infrastructure.

instructor.name: John Newsom
instructor.title: Assistant Professor of Practice
instructor.note: San Antonio native • UTSA alum • Air Force veteran • Former radio DJ

format.label: Live Sessions
format.value: Weekly Student Hours (Tuesdays 6 PM); otherwise asynchronous

cycleCallout.title: Module Structure
cycleCallout.body: Each module is structured as three Units. Unit 1 (Training) and Unit 2 (Training+) house the Foundation Labs; Unit 3 (Engagement) is where you deliver the Engagement Packet to the client. See Grading Info for details.

footer: Questions? Ask in Discord or schedule office hours.
